title: Gigantes Plaki

overview.description: A traditional Greek baked giant bean casserole cooked in a rich tomato sauce with herbs and olive oil. Simple pantry ingredients combine to create a hearty, satisfying vegetarian meal.

ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper
method: Heat the olive oil in a large ovenproof casserole or frying pan. Cook the onion and carrots for about 10 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and tomato purée and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes, stock, oregano, thyme and bay leaves.
Add the butter beans and simmer gently for 10 minutes until the sauce begins to thicken.
Transfer to an ovenproof dish if necessary and bake uncovered for 35–40 minutes until the sauce is rich and slightly caramelised.
Remove the bay leaves, stir through most of the parsley and season to taste.
Serve topped with the remaining parsley and crumbled feta if using.

learning.did-you-know: In Greece, gigantes are traditionally made with giant white beans called gigantes. Butter beans make an excellent and widely available substitute in the UK.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How slow baking transforms a simple tomato sauce into something rich and deeply flavoured, and how Mediterranean cooking relies on herbs and olive oil rather than heavy spices.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An excellent Pantry Monday meal that's inexpensive, healthy and relies almost entirely on store cupboard ingredients.
chapman-tips: Butter beans are an excellent substitute for traditional gigantes beans.
Don't rush the baking—the slight caramelisation gives the dish much of its flavour.
A drizzle of good olive oil just before serving lifts the whole dish.
notes.batch-cooking: Freezes exceptionally well. Portion into containers and thaw overnight before reheating gently.
notes.leftovers: Excellent reheated for lunch or served on toasted bread the following day.
